mappings.rationaleAI: The British Empire's Scramble for Africa, Pax Britannica, and colonial expansion relate to Egypt under British rule (B.1.b), the scramble for African colonies (D.1), establishment of European colonies in West Africa (D.2), and European penetration into Central Africa (D.5).

World War I's Ottoman participation and Middle Eastern theatres relate to the growth of Turkish nationalism and dissolution of the Ottoman Empire (A.2.c) and the postwar settlement encouraging Arab nationalism (B.3.a).

The Ottoman Empire from 1807 to 1920 including Mahmud II's reforms, Tanzimat reforms, Greek revolt, loss of territories, Young Turk Revolution, and WWI directly addresses the entire section A on Ottoman European intervention and westernization.

Egypt's Khedivate, British occupation, and post-1952 governance relate to the emergence of modern Egypt (B.1), centralized administration and construction of the Suez Canal (B.1.a), and Egypt under British rule (B.1.b).
Napoleon's Egyptian campaign (1798-1801) directly relates to Egypt under French occupation at the beginning of its modern emergence (B.1.a).
Algeria's French colonial annexation from 1830 directly addresses Algeria from 1830 to 1920 including the French conquest and national resistance under Abdelkader (B.2.a).

The Scramble for Africa's partition of the continent including the 1884 Berlin Conference directly addresses the imperialistic scramble for African colonies and the Berlin West Africa Conference (D.1).

Ethiopia's resistance to European colonialism under Tewodros II, Yohannes IV, and Menilek II directly addresses the consolidation of central government in Ethiopia and struggles against Egypt, Sudan, and Italy (D.3.b).
Nigeria's British colonization unifying Southern and Northern Protectorates in 1914 relates to the establishment of European colonies in West Africa (D.2).
The decline of the slave trade and its relationship to European commercial activities relate to the decline in the slave trade and European expansion (D.1).
The Middle East's coverage of Ottoman decline, Arab nationalism, and colonial legacy provides broad context for the Ottoman Empire section (A) and Egypt, Maghrib, and Arabian Peninsula (B).
The DRC's history under Leopold II's Congo Free State directly addresses King Leopold II's colonial enterprise in the Congo (D.5.b).
Turkey's history including Ataturk's secular republic relates to the growth of Turkish nationalism in the reign of Abdulhamid II and dissolution of the empire (A.2.c).
Russia's imperial expansion relates to Russian intrusions in Turkey during Mahmud II's era (A.1).
European colonial empires employing mercantilism and chartered companies directly relate to sub-Saharan Africa from c. 1885 to c. 1920 and the establishment of European colonial regimes (D).
Imperialism as the extension of power over foreign territories relates to the imperialistic scramble for African colonies (D.1).
African history's coverage of European colonial penetration and anti-colonial movements provides context for sub-Saharan Africa from c. 1885 to c. 1920 (D).
Italy's colonial expansion relates to the growth of Italian influence in Libya resulting in conquest (1911-12) (B.2.d).
